message:
test(fuzz): add ExecutionContext cancel/child/scope state-machine fuzz target (resolves lziow)

ExecutionContext (fcp_async_core/lib.rs:523-609) is the cancellation
+ deadline + scope primitive propagated through async work. cancel()
triggers via a shared CancellationToken (cloned into children at
lib.rs:561-567), so cancel-via-child also cancels the parent — this
is shared-token semantics, not strict downward propagation.

A regression that broke the shared cancellation would let a cancelled
request continue spawning work in its descendants — a compute-
exhaustion class of bug.

NOT covered by existing fuzz.

Six time-independent properties:
- initial state: !is_cancelled()
- cancel marks cancelled
- child inheritance: parent.cancel() → child.is_cancelled() = true
- idempotent cancel
- scope preservation through child()
- request_scoped has Some(deadline); background has None

Once-gated anchors:
- (a) parent.cancel() → child cancelled (shared-token propagation)
- (b) child.cancel() → parent cancelled (documented shared-token
  semantics; NOT strict downward-only)
- (c) request_scoped has deadline; background does not
- (d) with_deadline doesn't trigger cancellation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -815,5 +815,12 @@ test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_execution_context"
+path = "fuzz_targets/execution_context.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
 [workspace]
 members = ["."]
